paper_title: Graph Isomorphism for $(H_1,H_2)$-free Graphs: An Almost Complete Dichotomy
summary: The source paper itself represents major progress on this research question, resolving the computational complexity of Graph Isomorphism for $(H_1,H_2)$-free graphs for all but six pairs $(H_1,H_2)$, and was published in Algorithmica in 2021. No subsequent paper resolving the remaining six open cases has been found in the indexed literature through May 2026; the complete dichotomy for all pairs $(H_1,H_2)$ remains open.
search_queries: Graph Isomorphism H1 H2 free graphs dichotomy complexity Bonamy Bousquet Dabrowski Johnson Paulusma
graph isomorphism H-free graphs computational complexity dichotomy arXiv 1811.12252
graph isomorphism H1 H2 free graphs remaining open cases complete dichotomy 2020 2021 2022 2023 2024 2025
notes: The source paper (published in Algorithmica 2021, doi:10.1007/s00453-020-00747-x) settled all but six pairs (H1,H2). Schweitzer had previously proved that only finitely many cases remain open. Wide web search (Semantic Scholar, Paulusma's publication list, arXiv) found no follow-up paper resolving the remaining six cases; the complete dichotomy question is still open with high confidence.
